evals.prompt: We just finished a long session working on the portfolio site. We added a dark mode toggle to the navbar, decided to use localStorage over cookies (because the app is static and we don't want backend deps), and modified app/layout.tsx and components/Navbar.tsx. We still need to add a theme provider and write tests. Save our progress.
evals.expected_output: A .claude-memory.md file is created with a timestamped entry capturing: goal (dark mode toggle), status (in-progress), decisions (localStorage over cookies + reason), files (layout.tsx, Navbar.tsx), and next steps (theme provider + tests).

assertions.text: The entry contains 'in-progress' as the status
localStorage is mentioned alongside a reason (static / no backend)
Both layout.tsx and Navbar.tsx appear in the entry
Next steps mention theme provider AND tests
The memory entry body (excluding header) is under 300 words

evals.prompt: Load memory — I want to continue working on this project from where we left off.
evals.expected_output: Claude reads .claude-memory.md, outputs a clear resume briefing with: last session date, goal, status, key decisions, files touched, and a next-steps checklist. Ends with a prompt to continue.

assertions.text: A response.md file was produced with substantive content (over 50 words)
The briefing mentions the dark mode toggle goal
All three next steps (ThemeProvider, tests, WCAG check) are mentioned
The localStorage decision is explicitly mentioned in the briefing
The briefing ends with an invitation to continue (question or offer to proceed)

evals.prompt: We had a rough session today. We were trying to fix a TypeScript error in src/api/auth.ts — the req.user type wasn't being recognized. We tried module augmentation but it broke the build. Still unresolved. We also added a /health endpoint in src/api/routes.ts which worked fine. Save this.
evals.expected_output: Entry is appended with status 'blocked', the TS error is logged as unresolved under Errors, the health endpoint is logged under Files, and Next steps captures 'resolve req.user type error'.

assertions.text: The TS/req.user error is present and marked as unresolved
Both auth.ts and routes.ts appear in the entry
Next steps include resolving the TypeScript / req.user type issue
